---
title: "grant_terminations"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
```

```{r}
terminations_raw <- read_csv("https://files.grant-witness.us/nih_terminations.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("date"), ~ as_date(.x)))

county_commute <- read.csv("data/county_commute_NIH.csv") %>%
  select(DESTINATION, DESTINATION_total_workers, Commuters, ORIGIN, proportion) %>% 
  mutate(DESTINATION = str_pad(DESTINATION, 11, pad = "0"),
         ORIGIN = str_pad(ORIGIN, 5, pad = "0"))

cong_commute <- fread("https://osf.io/m5kb6/download") %>%
  mutate(Cong_DESTINATION = str_pad(w_tract, 11, pad = "0"),
         Cong_ORIGIN = str_pad(h_cd119, 4, pad = "0")) %>%
  rename(commuters = commutes) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Cong_ORIGIN))

cong_info <- read.csv("./data/us_representatives_119th_congress.csv") %>%
  mutate(GEOID = str_pad(GEOID, 4, pad = "0")) %>%
  select(GEOID, STATE_NAME, NAME, PARTY) %>%
  rename(state = STATE_NAME,
         pol_party = PARTY,
         rep_name = NAME)

# get total workers per cong district
cong_commute_sum <- cong_commute %>%
  group_by(Cong_DESTINATION) %>%
  summarise(total_workers = sum(commuters, na.rm=TRUE))

# merge in total workers
cong_commute <- left_join(cong_commute, cong_commute_sum) %>%
  select(Cong_DESTINATION, Cong_ORIGIN, commuters, total_workers) %>%
  rename(DESTINATION = Cong_DESTINATION,
         ORIGIN = Cong_ORIGIN)

# calculate proportion of workers who work in district X who live in district Y
cong_commute$proportion <- cong_commute$commuters / cong_commute$total_workers
```

```{r}
NIH_tracts <- read.csv("data/tract_dictionary.csv")
states <- read.csv("data/states.csv") %>%
  mutate(FIPS = str_pad(FIPS, 2, pad = "0"))

fy_start <- function(fy) {
  as_date(paste0(fy - 1, "-10-01"))
}

start_date <- as_date("2025-04-01")

dates_04_25 <- data.frame(
  date = seq(start_date, today(), by = "days"),
  day_fisc = seq(1, ceiling(time_length(today() - start_date, unit = "days")) + 1, by = 1)) %>%
  mutate(week = ceiling(day_fisc/7)) %>%
  group_by(week) %>%
  mutate(week_start = date[which.min(day_fisc)],
         week_end = date[which.max(day_fisc)]) %>%
  select(-date)

terminations <- terminations_raw %>%
  filter(org_state %in% states$state) %>%
  left_join(states %>% rename(org_state = state)) %>%
  rename(state_FIPS = FIPS) %>%
  left_join(NIH_tracts) %>%
  mutate(total_loss = total_award - total_estimated_outlays,
         total_loss = ifelse(total_loss < 0, 0, total_loss),
         across(ends_with("loss"), ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), 0, .x)),
         date_impacted = pmin(frozen_date, termination_date, na.rm = T),
         date_reinstated = pmax(unfrozen_date, reinstated_est_date, na.rm = T),
         tractid = ifelse(tractid == "00NOT_FOUND", NA, tractid)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(tractid)) %>%
  filter(date_impacted >= start_date) %>%
  # track reinstated grants as negative loss
  pivot_longer(c(date_impacted, date_reinstated), names_to = "date_status", values_to = "date") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(date)) %>%
  mutate(total_loss = if_else(date_status == "date_reinstated", -total_loss, total_loss), # total amount lost at the moment
         total_impacted = if_else(total_loss < 0, 0, total_loss), # total amount ever impacted
         day_fisc = time_length(date - start_date, unit = "days") + 1,
         week = ceiling(day_fisc/7))

# these grants have very little information about them and aren't included
missing_org_name <- terminations %>% 
  filter(is.na(org_name))

current_week <- ceiling(time_length(today() - start_date, unit = "weeks"))

# organization level
terminations_org <- terminations %>%
  complete(org_name, week = seq(1, current_week, by = 1),
           fill = list(total_loss = 0, total_impacted = 0)) %>%
  left_join(dates_04_25 %>% select(-c(day_fisc)) %>% distinct()) %>%
  select(-c(org_city, org_state)) %>%
  left_join(terminations %>% select(org_name, org_city, org_state) %>% distinct()) %>%
  group_by(org_name, org_city, org_state, week, week_start, week_end) %>%
  summarize(across(c("total_loss", "total_impacted"), ~ sum(.x))) %>%
  group_by(org_name, org_city, org_state) %>%
  arrange(week) %>%
  mutate(across(c("total_loss", "total_impacted"), ~ cumsum(.x))) %>%
  rename(current_loss = total_loss,
         overall_loss = total_impacted) %>%
  left_join(states %>% rename(org_state = state)) %>%
  mutate(current_econ_loss = current_loss * econ_mult,
         current_job_loss = current_loss * job_val,
         overall_econ_loss = overall_loss * econ_mult,
         overall_job_loss = overall_loss * job_val) %>%
  select(org_name, org_city, org_state, starts_with(c("week", "current", "overall")))

write_csv(terminations_org, "output/terminations_org.csv")

terminations_sum <- terminations %>%
  group_by(tractid, week) %>%
  summarise(week_loss = sum(total_loss),
            week_impacted = sum(total_impacted))

# add in commutes
terminations_sum_comm <- county_commute %>%
  rename(tractid = DESTINATION) %>%
  right_join(terminations_sum) %>%
  mutate(week_loss = week_loss * proportion,
         week_impacted = week_impacted * proportion) %>%
  group_by(ORIGIN, week) %>%
  summarize(week_loss = sum(week_loss),
            week_impacted = sum(week_impacted)) %>%
  rename(FIPS = ORIGIN) %>%
  filter(!endsWith(FIPS, "000")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  complete(FIPS, week = seq(1, current_week, by = 1),
           fill = list(week_loss = 0, week_impacted = 0)) %>%
  group_by(FIPS) %>%
  arrange(week) %>%
  mutate(current_loss = cumsum(week_loss),
         overall_loss = cumsum(week_impacted))

# add in state multipliers
terminations_sum_comm <- terminations_sum_comm %>%
  mutate(state_FIPS = str_sub(FIPS, 1, 2)) %>%
  left_join(states %>% rename(state_FIPS = FIPS)) %>%
  mutate(current_econ_loss = current_loss * econ_mult,
         current_job_loss = current_loss * job_val,
         overall_econ_loss = overall_loss * econ_mult,
         overall_job_loss = overall_loss * job_val) %>%
  left_join(dates_04_25 %>% select(-c(day_fisc)) %>% distinct()) %>%
  select(FIPS, week, week_start, week_end, starts_with(c("current", "overall")))

write_csv(terminations_sum_comm, "output/terminations_county.csv")

terminations_sum_comm %>%
  group_by(state, week, week_start, week_end) %>%
  summarise(across(ends_with("loss"), ~ sum(.x))) %>%
  write_csv("output/terminations_state.csv")

msa <- read.csv("data/cbsa2fipsxw_2023.csv") %>%
  select(CountyFIPS, CBSA_NAME, CBSA_FIPS) %>%
  rename(FIPS = CountyFIPS) %>%
  mutate(FIPS = str_pad(FIPS, 5, pad ="0"),
         CBSA_FIPS = str_pad(CBSA_FIPS, 5, pad ="0"))

terminations_sum_comm %>%
  left_join(msa) %>%
  group_by(CBSA_FIPS, CBSA_NAME, week, week_start, week_end) %>%
  summarise(across(ends_with("loss"), ~ sum(.x))) %>%
  write_csv("output/terminations_city.csv")

# congressional district
terminations_sum_cong <- cong_commute %>%
  rename(tractid = DESTINATION) %>%
  right_join(terminations_sum) %>%
  mutate(week_loss = week_loss * proportion,
         week_impacted = week_impacted * proportion) %>%
  group_by(ORIGIN, week) %>%
  summarize(week_loss = sum(week_loss),
            week_impacted = sum(week_impacted)) %>%
  rename(GEOID = ORIGIN) %>%
  filter(!endsWith(GEOID, "000")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  complete(GEOID, week = seq(1, current_week, by = 1),
           fill = list(week_loss = 0, week_impacted = 0)) %>%
  group_by(GEOID) %>%
  arrange(week) %>%
  mutate(current_loss = cumsum(week_loss),
         overall_loss = cumsum(week_impacted))

terminations_sum_cong <- terminations_sum_cong %>%
  mutate(state_FIPS = str_sub(GEOID, 1, 2)) %>%
  left_join(states %>% rename(state_FIPS = FIPS)) %>%
  mutate(current_econ_loss = current_loss * econ_mult,
         current_job_loss = current_loss * job_val,
         overall_econ_loss = overall_loss * econ_mult,
         overall_job_loss = overall_loss * job_val) %>%
  left_join(dates_04_25 %>% select(-c(day_fisc)) %>% distinct()) %>%
  select(GEOID, week, week_start, week_end, starts_with(c("current", "overall")))

write_csv(terminations_sum_cong, "output/terminations_district.csv")

# all US terminations
terminations_sum_comm %>%
  group_by(week_start) %>%
  summarise(current_loss = sum(current_loss),
            overall_loss = sum(overall_loss)) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = week_start, y = current_loss)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = week_start, y = overall_loss))

```
